description.s: Code items in Hobson are evaluated as native ES modules using dynamic
. This provides isolation, strict mode safety, and standard JavaScript module semantics.
— code runs in its own scope, not global scope
— catches common errors, prevents unsafe operations
is natively supported in all modern browsers
There's no module resolution system (no CDN, no node_modules). Code lives in IndexedDB. Blob URLs let the browser's native
Added explicitly as a safety net:
comment that maps blob URLs back to item names in browser DevTools:
This means stack traces show readable item names instead of opaque blob URLs.
Third-party libraries are stored as code items:
This maintains offline-first operation — all code is local in IndexedDB, no network needed.
Download the library's ES module source
, wrapping if needed for proper exports
This is a personal system — isolation protects against
Defending against malicious code would add massive complexity
The goal is safety from mistakes, not a security sandbox
Circular dependencies across items are not allowed. Detection via call stack tracking during
throws a clear error showing the circular chain. Mutually recursive functions must be in the same code item.
